top_stories.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
top_stories.summary: LastPass confirmed a data breach in which hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment by stealing the company's OAuth tokens via the Klue supply chain attack. The breach highlights supply chain vulnerabilities affecting OAuth token security at a major password management and identity vendor.

top_stories.title: Omada Introduces Omada Identity Sovereign, Bringing Digital Sovereignty for Regulated Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Identity Sovereign, a new solution designed for regulated European organizations that allows them to deploy and operate identity governance on infrastructure of their own choosing. The product is specifically engineered to ensure no non-European entity is involved in the chain, addressing digital sovereignty requirements for regulated industries in Europe.

top_stories.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Highlights Software Development Pipeline Risk
top_stories.summary: A GitHub token leak at Novo Nordisk exposed vulnerabilities in software development pipeline security, specifically around secrets management. The incident illustrates how organizations misclassify secrets management as a tooling problem rather than an identity and access management problem.

top_stories.title: What the Fortibleed campaign means for organizations running FortiGate firewalls
top_stories.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting campaign called "FortiBleed" targeted FortiGate firewalls, exposing thousands of organizations to network compromise including full domain-level control in some cases. Researchers from ZenoX and CloudSEK analyzed attacker tools, scripts, and credentials inadvertently left exposed on a server, revealing a highly automated attack pipeline.

top_stories.title: FortiBleed Targeted FortiGate Firewalls in 110 Million-Credential Harvesting Operation
top_stories.summary: A Russian-speaking initial access broker conducted a large-scale credential-harvesting operation called FortiBleed, targeting over 430,000 FortiGate firewalls globally and collecting 110 million credentials. The campaign, active since February 2026, involves credential list collection, exposed service scanning, brute-forcing, and custom tool deployment against FortiGate infrastructure.

news.title: Scope of Salesforce Attacks Expands as Icarus Leaks Data
news.summary: Attackers breached application vendor Klue and used stolen OAuth tokens to access and exfiltrate customer Salesforce data, with the scope of victims expanding. The attack vector involves OAuth token abuse, a key authentication and authorization mechanism used to delegate access between applications.

news.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
news.summary: Threat actors exploited FortiGate firewalls using a Golang-based credential-stealing sniffer in an ongoing global campaign. The attack targeted 430,000 FortiGate devices and resulted in the identification of 110 million credentials.

news.title: Trump Order Sets 2030 Deadline for Federal Post-Quantum Crypto Migration
news.summary: President Trump signed Executive Order 14409 on June 22, requiring federal agencies to migrate high-value assets and high-impact systems to post-quantum cryptography by December 31, 2030, with digital signatures deadline set for December 31, 2031. National security systems are handled on a separate track under the order.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for sophisticated social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise major organizations.

news.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Got Stolen Anyway
news.summary: The article covers SIM swap attacks as a method for stealing one-time passwords (OTPs) delivered via SMS, leading to account takeovers. The piece highlights the vulnerability of SMS-based MFA and the need for stronger authentication methods.

news.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
news.summary: A large-scale campaign dubbed "FortiBleed" targeted Fortinet FortiGate devices using custom sniffers to harvest authentication credentials from compromised firewalls. SOCRadar reported the attack involved stealing authentication secrets from the network security devices.

news.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
news.summary: A 29-year-old heap over-read vulnerability dubbed "Squidbleed" in the Squid web proxy can leak cleartext HTTP requests, including credentials and session tokens, to other users sharing the same proxy. The flaw originates from a 1997 FTP-parsing change and remains present in Squid's default configuration, disclosed by Calif.io researchers in June.

news.title: Microsoft to retire Azure DevOps issuer for workload identity federation
news.summary: Microsoft has announced the deprecation of the Azure DevOps issuer for workload identity federation service connections, with retirement set for July 1, 2027. The change is part of an initiative to standardize on the Microsoft Entra issuer for workload identity federation.

news.title: Navigating the AI access control minefield
news.summary: The article addresses the challenge of implementing access control for AI systems that require access to enterprise data. It frames AI access management as an emerging concern for IT leaders, drawing parallels to early e-commerce security challenges.

news.title: 生成AI型AI-OCR「GenOCR」、SAML 認証および Microsoft Entra ID による SSO に対応 ― ユーザーのセキュリティ強化と利便性向上へ
news.summary: GenOCR, a generative AI-based OCR product by Jinbei, has added support for SAML authentication and SSO via Microsoft Entra ID. The update is aimed at improving security and user convenience for enterprise customers.

news.title: Show HN: Lelu – authorization engine that catches manipulated AI agents
news.summary: An open-source authorization engine called Lelu has been released on GitHub, targeting AI agent access control. The project includes features such as confidence-aware gating, human-in-the-loop review, policy-as-code, and audit trail capabilities for controlling manipulated AI agents.

news.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
news.summary: This article argues that shadow AI has evolved from a data leakage problem into an access control problem, shifting the focus toward IAM concerns. The piece addresses how unsanctioned AI tools interacting with enterprise systems create authorization and identity management risks.

news.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
news.summary: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration after a security incident involving OAuth token abuse exposed customer data on June 11, 2026. The incident resulted in organizations being unable to connect to Salesforce via the Klue app until further notice, highlighting risks associated with OAuth-based third-party integrations.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems allowed potential remote takeover of World Cup streams due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The bug exposed how misconfigured identity and access management policies could have enabled unauthorized control over live broadcast infrastructure.

news.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
news.summary: The article addresses the identity governance challenge of orphaned AI agents that retain standing privileges after their creators leave an organization. It focuses on the lack of accountability and authorization tracking for autonomous AI agents accessing enterprise systems, framing it as an access management and identity governance problem.

news.title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
news.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has released a stable Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension that enables centralized provisioning of MCP server access through an organization's identity provider. The extension eliminates per-app OAuth flows by allowing users to receive pre-connected servers automatically upon first login, integrating AI tool access into existing IAM infrastructure.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A credential-harvesting campaign has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices, collecting working credentials across nearly 200 countries. The attack targets multiple sectors and has resulted in a large compiled database of valid credentials from the affected devices.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force accounts. Instructions circulating on Telegram demonstrated how to social-engineer the AI bot into bypassing normal account authentication and recovery controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, causing a credentials breach that CISA is still working to contain. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers from CISA as the agency works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credentials and access management failure involving government infrastructure.
